article:
In brief: Today in Abuja, we're expecting a very warm day with plenty of sunshine, though the heat index will make it feel more like 30°C. As we look ahead to the rest of the week, our attention turns to an increasing chance of rain, which could impact your commute, particularly if you're planning on driving along the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway or visiting the National Assembly Complex. We'll break down the details of the forecast, day by day, to help you plan your week.

First, let's talk about the current conditions. It's a pleasant morning in Abuja, with a temperature of 26°C, though it already feels like 30°C due to the high humidity. The wind is light, blowing at just 6 km/h, which means it's not providing much relief from the heat. If you're planning on spending time outdoors today, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're visiting the Millennium Park or the National Arboretum.

As we look at the UV Index, it's currently at 0, which is unusual for this time of day. However, this is likely due to the time of year and the fact that the sun is still relatively low in the sky. As the day wears on, the UV Index will likely increase, so be sure to take necessary precautions if you plan on spending time outdoors. If you're driving, you'll want to be aware of the traffic conditions, and fortunately, the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently experiencing light traffic, with speeds of 38 km/h.

### Thursday, April 16
Today's forecast is calling for a high of 34°C, with just a 15% chance of rain. This means it's likely to be a dry and sunny day, perfect for getting outdoors and enjoying the city. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Mosque or the National Christian Centre, you can expect plenty of sunshine, but be sure to stay cool and hydrated. As you're out and about, keep an eye on the skies, as there is a slight chance of an isolated shower. However, it's unlikely to impact your plans significantly.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will dip to a low of 26°C, providing some relief from the heat of the day. If you're planning on dining outdoors at one of the many restaurants in the Jabi Lake area, you can expect a pleasant evening, with plenty of stars visible in the sky. Just be sure to bring a light jacket, as it can cool down significantly once the sun sets.

### Friday, April 17
Tomorrow, our attention turns to an increasing chance of rain, with a 45% chance of showers and thunderstorms. This means that if you're planning on driving to work or school, you'll want to leave a little extra time to account for potentially slow traffic. The Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway could become congested, especially if there are any accidents or incidents related to the weather. Be sure to check the latest traffic updates before you head out, and consider alternative routes if possible.

The high temperature will reach 35°C, which is just a degree warmer than today. However, the increased chance of rain means that it's likely to feel more humid, so be sure to stay cool and hydrated. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja Market, you may want to consider bringing an umbrella or rain jacket, as there is a good chance of showers.

### Saturday, April 18
As we head into the weekend, the chance of rain increases to 55%. This means that it's likely to be a wet and stormy day, with plenty of showers and thunderstorms around. If you're planning on outdoor activities, such as visiting the Aso Rock or the Three Arms Zone, you may want to consider rescheduling for another day. The rain could make the roads slippery, so be sure to exercise caution if you need to drive.

The high temperature will dip to 31°C, which is a few degrees cooler than the past few days. However, the increased chance of rain means that it's likely to feel more humid, so be sure to stay cool and hydrated. If you're planning on dining outdoors, you may want to consider indoor seating options, as the rain could make outdoor dining uncomfortable.

### Sunday, April 19
On Sunday, the chance of rain increases to 68%, making it the wettest day of the week. This means that it's likely to be a stormy day, with plenty of showers and thunderstorms around. If you're planning on driving to church or other activities, be sure to leave plenty of time to account for potentially slow traffic. The roads could become congested, especially if there are any accidents or incidents related to the weather.

The high temperature will reach 33°C, which is a few degrees warmer than the past few days. However, the increased chance of rain means that it's likely to feel more humid, so be sure to stay cool and hydrated. If you're planning on visiting the National Museum, you may want to consider indoor activities, as the rain could make outdoor exhibits uncomfortable.

### Monday, April 20
As we head into the new week, the chance of rain decreases slightly to 55%. This means that it's still likely to be a wet day, with plenty of showers and thunderstorms around. If you're planning on driving to work or school, be sure to leave a little extra time to account for potentially slow traffic. The Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway could become congested, especially if there are any accidents or incidents related to the weather.

The high temperature will reach 33°C, which is similar to the past few days. However, the decreased chance of rain means that it's likely to feel less humid, so be sure to stay cool and hydrated. If you're planning on outdoor activities, such as visiting the Jabi Lake, you may want to consider bringing an umbrella or rain jacket, as there is still a good chance of showers.

### Tuesday, April 21
On Tuesday, the chance of rain decreases significantly to just 5%. This means that it's likely to be a dry and sunny day, perfect for getting outdoors and enjoying the city. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Mosque or the National Christian Centre, you can expect plenty of sunshine, but be sure to stay cool and hydrated. As you're out and about, keep an eye on the skies, as there is a slight chance of an isolated shower. However, it's unlikely to impact your plans significantly.

The high temperature will reach 36°C, which is the warmest day of the week. Be sure to stay cool and hydrated, especially if you're planning on outdoor activities. If you're driving, be aware of the traffic conditions, and consider avoiding the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway during peak hours.

### Wednesday, April 22
Finally, on Wednesday, the chance of rain remains at just 5%. This means that it's likely to be another dry and sunny day, perfect for getting outdoors and enjoying the city. If you're planning on visiting the National Assembly Complex or the Supreme Court, you can expect plenty of sunshine, but be sure to stay cool and hydrated. As you're out and about, keep an eye on the skies, as there is a slight chance of an isolated shower. However, it's unlikely to impact your plans significantly.

The high temperature will reach 35°C, which is similar to the past few days. Be sure to stay cool and hydrated, especially if you're planning on outdoor activities. If you're driving, be aware of the traffic conditions, and consider avoiding the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway during peak hours. In conclusion, this week's forecast is calling for a mix of sun and rain, with the chance of showers and thunderstorms increasing mid-week. Be sure to stay cool and hydrated, and plan your activities accordingly. If you have any questions or concerns about the forecast, feel free to reach out to us. We're always here to help.
